Roo Code Installation Guide

Step-by-step instructions for setting up Roo Code in VS Code:

Introduction

Roo Code is a powerful AI-powered VS Code: extension that brings a whole dev team of AI agents directly into your editor. With built-in and extensible modes, Roo Code: helps you plan, architect, code, debug, and boost your productivity like never before.

This guide will walk you through the complete installation and setup process.

Prerequisites

Before installing Roo Code:, ensure you have:

  • Visual Studio Code: installed (latest version recommended)
  • An API key from a supported LLM provider (e.g., OpenRouter, OpenAI, Anthropic)

Step 1: Install Roo Code: Extension

The first step is to install the Roo Code: extension from the VS Code: Marketplace.

Instructions

  1. Open VS Code:
  2. Click on the Extensions icon in the left sidebar (or press Ctrl+Shift+X)
  3. In the search box, type roo
  4. Look for “Roo Code:” by RooVeterinaryInc
  5. Click the Install button

Verification

After installation, you should see the Roo Code: icon (a kangaroo silhouette) appear in your left sidebar.

Step 2: Choose Account Option

Once installed, Roo Code: will display a welcome screen with account options.

Instructions

  1. The welcome screen will prompt you to create a Roo Code: Cloud account
  2. For immediate use without creating an account, click on “or use without an account”
Account vs. No Account
  • With Account: Get powerful models, web control, analytics, support, and more
  • Without Account: Use immediately with your own API keys from third-party providers

Step 3: Configure LLM Provider

Roo Code: requires an LLM provider to function. You’ll need to configure your provider settings.

Instructions

  1. On the “Choose your provider” screen, select “3rd-party Provider”
  2. Select your API Provider from the dropdown (e.g., OpenRouter)
  3. Enter your API Key in the provided field
  4. Select your desired Model (e.g., google/gemini-2.5-flash-preview)
  5. Click “Finish” to complete the setup

Getting an API Key

An OpenRouter API key will be provided to you. Here’s how to use it:

  1. Copy the provided API key
  2. Paste it into the “OpenRouter API Key” text box
API Key Format

OpenRouter API keys start with sk-or-v1- followed by a long string of characters. For example:

sk-or-v1-abc123def456ghi789jkl012mno345pqr678stu901vwx234yz
Security Note

Your API keys are stored securely in VS Code:’s Secret Storage and are never shared.

Choosing a Model

⚠️ Important: Change the Default Model!

The default model is Claude Sonnet, which is very expensive and will drain your credits quickly. Always change this to a more cost-effective option before starting.

Recommended Models

Start with one of these cost-effective models:

  • google/gemini-3-flash
  • glm-5
  • kimi-k2.5

Cost-Effective Workflow

  1. Start with Gemini 3 Flash — it’s fast and inexpensive for most coding tasks
  2. If the model struggles with complex logic or repeatedly fails to complete a task correctly, try a more capable model like GLM-5 or Kimi K2.5
  3. Upgrade when necessary — move to stronger models only when cheaper ones fail after multiple attempts
Pro Tip

You can change models anytime in Roo Code: settings. Start with Gemini 3 Flash and only upgrade when necessary to manage costs effectively.

Step 4: Open a Workspace Folder

Roo Code: works best when you have a folder open in VS Code:.

Instructions

  1. Click on File in the top menu
  2. Select Open Folder… (or press Ctrl+K Ctrl+O)
  3. Choose the project folder you want to work on (or create a new folder for a new project)
  4. Click Select Folder

Why Opening a Folder is Essential

Roo Code: is an AI coding agent that needs context about your project to provide effective assistance:

For Existing Projects

  • Code Understanding: Roo Code: can read your existing files to understand the project structure, dependencies, and coding patterns
  • Consistent Style: It matches your existing code style and conventions
  • Accurate Refactoring: Changes are made with full awareness of the codebase

For New Projects

  • Project Structure: Roo Code: can create proper folder structures and initialize files in the right locations
  • File Management: It can create, modify, and organize multiple files automatically
  • Context Awareness: Even for new projects, having a workspace folder allows Roo Code: to track what files exist and manage the project holistically

What Happens Without a Folder Open

  • Roo Code: operates in a limited mode without file system access
  • It cannot read existing code or create new files
  • Many features become unavailable or less effective
Best Practice

Always open a folder before using Roo Code:, even for brand new projects. Create an empty folder if needed — this gives Roo Code: a workspace to operate in.

Getting Started with Roo Code:

Now that Roo Code: is installed and configured, you can:

Available Modes

Roo Code: comes with several built-in modes:

Mode Description
Code Write and modify code with AI assistance
Architect Plan and design system architecture
Ask Get explanations and answers about your code
Debug Diagnose and fix issues
Custom Create your own specialized modes

Basic Usage

  1. Click on the Roo Code: icon in the left sidebar
  2. Type your request in natural language
  3. Roo Code: will generate code, explanations, or perform tasks based on your instructions

Troubleshooting

Common Issues

API Key Not Working

  • Verify your API key is correct and active
  • Check that your provider account has available credits
  • Ensure you’ve selected the correct provider in settings

Roo Code: Not Responding

  • Check your internet connection
  • Verify the extension is enabled in VS Code:
  • Try reloading the VS Code: window (Ctrl+Shift+P → “Developer: Reload Window”)

Model Not Available

  • Some models require specific provider subscriptions
  • Try switching to a different model in the settings

Getting Help

  • Discord: Join the Roo Code: Discord community for support
  • Reddit: Visit r/RooCode for discussions
  • Documentation: Check the official VS Code: Marketplace page

Summary

Congratulations! You’ve successfully installed and configured Roo Code:. Here’s what we covered:

  1. ✅ Installed the Roo Code: extension from VS Code: Marketplace
  2. ✅ Chose to use Roo Code: without an account
  3. ✅ Configured a 3rd-party LLM provider with API key
  4. ✅ Opened a workspace folder for context

You’re now ready to boost your productivity with AI-powered coding assistance!

Happy Coding with Roo Code! 🦘
